title: ipomoea batatas var. littoralis
intro: Ipomoea batatas var. littoralis, also known as the beach potato or littoral sweet potato, is a variety of sweet potato that grows in coastal areas and is characterized by its ability to thrive in saline environments. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ipomoea batatas var. littoralis include its ability to reduce blood sugar levels, improve wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
